name: Core Dumped
oneLine: Animated explainer channel that turns operating systems and computer architecture topics into clean PowerPoint-style visuals.
bio: Core Dumped is run by George, a CS engineer based in Ecuador, who explains low-level computer-science concepts with simple animated diagrams. Production is famously homegrown: animations are built in PowerPoint and voice-over uses ElevenLabs AI. The channel (340k+ subscribers) is organised around two flagship playlists - Operating Systems Theory and Computer Architecture from Scratch - covering scheduling, paging, concurrency, caches, and pipelining.
style: Animated explainers around 8-15 minutes built in PowerPoint with AI voice-over, anchored by a recurring cartoon CPU/process character.
notable: Concurrency Is Not What You Think
The Fancy Algorithms That Make Your Computer Feel Smoother
